data.title: Charlotte's Web Announces Transaction with BAT: Debenture Conversion and US$10M Equity Investment to Strengthen Balance Sheet
data.description: Louisville, Colorado - On March 30, 2026, Charlotte's Web Holdings, Inc. announced a transaction that addresses its capital structure, facilitating support for near-term operating priorities. The company, listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange as CWEB and on the OTCQB as CWBHF, is preparing to participate in the anticipated CMMI Medicare Pilot Program. This move signifies an important step for the company as it aligns its financial position with upcoming strategic initiatives.

data.title: Suspected hacker behind $54M Uranium Finance hack may be sentenced to 30 years for allegedly buying collectibles.
data.description: Authorities claim that the embezzled money, totaling a significant sum of funds, was utilized in acquiring various collectibles such as Pokémon cards, antique Roman coins, and even a fragment of fabric from the historic aircraft of the Wright brothers.

data.title: Bitcoin could crash as oil prices hit 3-year high over $105, as historical data suggests deeper bear markets during such rallies.
data.description: Historical trends indicate that Bitcoin bear markets tend to worsen when oil prices surge to unprecedented levels. With WTI prices hitting $105 on Monday, there is concern that this spike could trigger a crash in BTC value.

data.title: Trump Willing To End Iran War Without Strait Of Hormuz Reopening: WSJ
data.description: According to a report by the Wall Street Journal, President Donald Trump has conveyed to his advisors his readiness to conclude the conflict with Iran, even if the Strait of Hormuz stays shut. The news suggests that Trump is open to ending the confrontation with Iran irrespective of the strategic waterway's closure. The report did not specify the source of this information, but it was highlighted in Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: America's 250th Kicks Off with 250,000 Idaho Potatoes in Record-Breaking Display
data.description: In Mitchell, South Dakota, on March 30, 2026, an event marked the United States' 250th birthday by revealing the world's biggest Idaho® potato exhibit. The Idaho Potato Commission (IPC) and County Fair Foods collaborated to construct the display using a staggering 250,000 Idaho potatoes. This exhibit served as a patriotic homage to America.
